assertion_text: Exhaustive matrix covers `tldr churn` and `tldr hotspots` × 18 languages. Both commands are already language-universal in source: churn.rs is pure git-log analysis with no language filter; hotspots.rs uses Language::from_path(...).is_none() => continue (unified VAL-008 detector). The gap is FIXTURE: canonical build_<lang> helpers create bare directories. Fix: add build_git_fixture helper that wraps build_fixture with git init + 3 commits, then add 36 tests (churn × 18 + hotspots × 18). No source changes to churn or hotspots.

helper_added.name: build_git_fixture
helper_added.behavior_summary: Calls build_fixture(lang, root) for the canonical 2-file 3-function fixture, then `git init --initial-branch=main` and creates 3 commits ('initial', 'touch1', 'touch2') with deterministic local-only git identity (GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL='', GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM='', inline GIT_AUTHOR_*/GIT_COMMITTER_* env vars). Each touch commit appends a single language-appropriate comment line (// or # or -- or (* *)) to the entry file. 3 commits is required to satisfy `tldr hotspots`'s default min_commits=3 (hotspots.rs:387) so the hotspots array is non-empty for every language.

shape_assertions.churn: ChurnReport.files (Vec<FileChurn>) field present AND non-empty (≥1 entry)
shape_assertions.hotspots: HotspotsReport.hotspots (Vec<HotspotEntry>) field present AND non-empty (≥1 entry)

verification_counts.clippy_on_touched_files: clean (cargo clippy -p tldr-cli --features semantic --test exhaustive_matrix -- -D warnings produces no warnings)

real_cli_repros.fixture_construction: build_git_fixture-equivalent bash: git init + 3 commits with same env-var sandbox as the helper
real_cli_repros.interpretation: All 3 languages produce non-empty churn.files and hotspots.hotspots, with the entry file (touched by all 3 commits) ranked first. Hotspots reports a single entry per repo because only the entry file clears the default min_commits=3 threshold (manifest + util are touched only by the initial commit).

constraints_honored.no_gaming: no #[allow], no weakened assertions, no #[cfg(test)] hide-issues
constraints_honored.no_source_changes_to_churn_or_hotspots: VERIFIED: `git diff crates/tldr-core/src/quality/churn.rs crates/tldr-core/src/quality/hotspots.rs crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/churn.rs crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/hotspots.rs` returns no output (those files unchanged)
constraints_honored.no_global_git_config_writes: build_git_fixture sets GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL='', GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM='', and uses `git config --local` for repo-scoped identity; never writes to ~/.gitconfig
constraints_honored.deterministic: Same lang input produces identical 3-commit history (same author email/name/timestamp via env vars, same comment text)
constraints_honored.preexisting_files_untouched: 111 pre-existing uncommitted files in working tree NOT modified by this commit; only the 4 explicitly listed files staged

clippy_unrelated.context: literal `3.14` used in a test assertion; pre-existing, not in VAL-017 scope
`result.findings.len() >= 1` should be `!result.findings.is_empty()`; pre-existing, not in VAL-017 scope
indexed for-loops; pre-existing, not in VAL-017 scope

preexisting_breakage_observed.test_unrelated: tldr-core lib at HEAD now reports 4755 passed / 0 failed (better than M13's note about a pre-existing surface::typescript failure — that failure has been addressed by an unrelated milestone).

phase_1_red.evidence: cargo build -p tldr-cli --tests --features semantic --release failed with `cannot find function build_git_fixture in module fixtures` (expected RED)

phase_2_green.evidence: cargo test -p tldr-cli --test exhaustive_matrix --features semantic --release -- test_churn_on test_hotspots_on -> 36 passed; 0 failed

phase_3_full_verify.evidence: Full exhaustive matrix 730/0/0; language_command_matrix 234/0/0; tldr-core 4755/0/356; clippy clean on touched files

phase_4_report_and_commit.evidence: M13 addendum + this JSON written; commit pending after report finalisation
